behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned
behaviors.justification: A unit test mocking get_changed_files() to return a file list will always pass. Real failures live in: git diff parsing (wrong ref/command), GitHub Actions context integration (wrong environment vars, wrong API call format), or filtering logic against actual git data. Must test with either real git operations or properly integrated GitHub API mocking.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a git repo with multiple committed files on main, create a feature branch with changes to a subset of files, integrate test with GitHub Actions context (mock or simulate), run linter, verify: (a) changed files are analyzed, (b) unchanged files are not touched, (c) git diff command executes correctly

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment
behaviors.justification: The grouping logic itself could be unit tested, but real failures depend on: (1) actual linter output format and severity field values, (2) GitHub API comment format requirements, (3) markdown/formatting correctness in posted comment. Mocking just the linter result and testing grouping logic misses API integration and formatting bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run actual linter on realistic sample code files, capture real findings with severity labels, execute grouping logic, mock GitHub API to intercept comment POST, verify: (a) findings correctly grouped into error/warning/info sections, (b) GitHub API call format is correct, (c) posted comment markdown renders properly with grouping visible

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout
behaviors.justification: Performance and timeout requirements cannot be tested with mocks — mocks eliminate real I/O, real linter subprocess execution, real filesystem access. A mocked linter will always complete instantly, hiding actual bottlenecks (file I/O, linter startup, GitHub API calls). Must measure real execution on realistic workload.
behaviors.test_description: Generate 200 realistic source files (varied sizes and types), run actual linter tool with real subprocess execution, measure total wall-clock time from start to completion, assert time < 10 minutes with margin, verify memory usage stays reasonable, check for I/O bottlenecks or linter process hangs

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted
behaviors.justification: Unit test of exception handling logic will pass with mocked linter calls. Real failures depend on: (1) actual linter crash behavior on real malformed input, (2) exception type and format the actual tool produces, (3) recovery logic working with real subprocess error codes, (4) remaining files actually being processed and results posted. Mocking the linter's exception hides whether real crash is actually handled.
behaviors.test_description: Create or obtain a genuinely malformed source file that triggers linter crash, either run real linter or mock subprocess linter to return error/crash exit code, execute pipeline, verify: (a) exception/error is caught and logged, (b) crash does not halt remaining file processing, (c) results for analyzed files are posted to PR comment anyway, (d) error is reported in comment or logs

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output
behaviors.justification: Deduplication logic itself is independent of how findings are produced. Real failure is in the dedup function: incorrect comparison, wrong key for matching, non-duplicate removal. Can be tested with mock findings (same line, message, different rule origin). Real linter producing overlapping findings is orthogonal to whether dedup logic works.
behaviors.test_description: Create mock findings list where two different linting rules both flag the same line with identical message/severity, run dedup function, verify: (a) duplicate is removed (count goes from 2 to 1), (b) non-duplicates on different lines remain, (c) findings with same line but different messages are not deduplicated, (d) edge cases (null severity, missing line) handled
